item.title: South Korea’s Policy Chief Flags Property Risks From Chip Windfall
item.description: South Korea’s presidential policy chief said policymakers need to consider how gains from the country’s chip-led boom will spread through the broader economy, warning that excess liquidity has historically found its way into the property market.

item.title: アップル変革のカギはデザイン復権、大ヒット左右する次期CEOの最優先課題－Power On
item.description: かつて世界を魅了したアップルのデザイン。次期CEOに託されたのは岐路に立つデザイン部門の再建だ。

item.title: China Warns of Fragmented Supply Chains as Trade Tensions Grow
item.description: China warned that supply chains may become disconnected as key trading partners mull new measures to address growing imbalances and reduce rare earths dependencies.

item.title: Rupee May Get Further Boost as Indian Banks Line Up Overseas Debt
item.description: A Nifty gauge of lenders has risen about 6% since June 5.

item.title: Qatar Domestic Gas Plant Malfunction Ignites Blast; 18 Missing
item.description: Qatar said an incident during the startup of the Ras Laffan industrial complex resulted in a blast that has injured dozens.

item.title: Masan Plans Tungsten Mine Expansion in Vietnam as It Courts Foreign Capital
item.description: Masan High-Tech Materials announced an expansion of its Nui Phao mine and Nui Chiem area, potentially adding 115 million tons of tungsten-polymetallic resources as the company seeks to attract foreign investors and establish a stronger foothold in the global critical minerals supply chain.

item.title: Chinese Stocks in Hong Kong Near Bear Market After Holiday
item.description: A gauge of Chinese stocks in Hong Kong edged toward a bear market as trading resumed after the holiday, weighed by weak consumption data and investors chasing AI shares elsewhere.

item.title: China Places Two US Rare Earths Producers on Export Control List
item.description: China has imposed export controls on two US rare earth producers that are part of Washington’s effort to establish alternative supply chains for minerals critical to advanced manufacturing and defense.

item.title: Qatar Rushes to Bring Back Empty LNG Ships as It Lifts Exports
item.description: Qatar is seeking to bring more empty liquefied natural gas tankers back home, as the nation aims to return about a fifth of global supply.

item.title: Trump Ally Wins Colombia Election, Heralding Pro-Market Turn
item.description: Colombia elected conservative lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella president on Sunday, heralding a swing back to business-friendly and pro-US policies after four years of leftist rule.

item.title: Japan’s Takaichi Signals Acceptance of BOJ’s Latest Rate Hike
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi signaled her acceptance of the Bank of Japan’s interest rate increase last week by reiterating a standard line touting government coordination with the central bank.

item.title: Pound Trades Near 2026 Low as UK Political Uncertainty Builds
item.description: The pound traded near this year’s low on expectations Keir Starmer will set out a timetable for his departure as UK prime minister in coming days.

item.title: Vale Board Resists Shareholder Previ’s Bid to Oust Chairman
item.description: Board members of Vale SA, the world’s top iron ore producer, voted against a proposal by one of the company’s largest shareholders to remove Daniel André Stieler as chairman, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Hong Kong Seeks More Investment Quotas, IPO Access for Chinese
item.description: Hong Kong is in talks with Chinese authorities to expand cross-border investment channels and grant mainland buyers access to local initial public offerings, following a regulatory crackdown on illicit offshore capital flows, according to Financial Secretary Paul Chan.

item.title: Treasuries Decline as Trump’s Iran Threats Stoke Inflation Fears
item.description: Treasuries fell after President Donald Trump again threatened military action against Iran over Hezbollah’s attacks on Israel, sending oil prices higher and prompting investors to reassess inflation risks.

item.title: Inghams Shares Tumble as Farms Locked Down in Fight Against H5N1
item.description: Shares in Australia-listed chicken producer Inghams Group Ltd. dropped as much as 14% in early Monday trading after the company said it had locked down its Western Australia operations following the detection of the H5N1 avian influenza in the state.

item.title: Abaxx Calls for Probe, Hires Paul Weiss After Short Seller’s Attack
item.description: Abaxx Technologies Inc. is asking Canadian market regulators to investigate whether manipulative trading occurred in its shares, and has hired law firm Paul Weiss for help in response to a short-selling campaign by Viceroy Research.

item.title: S. Korea’s Early Exports Jump Again as AI Boom Fuels Chip Demand
item.description: South Korea’s export growth remained robust in early June thanks to a continued boom in the semiconductor sector.

item.title: Gold Holds Decline After Trump Warns Iran During Peace Talks
item.description: Gold held a decline after US President Donald Trump issued a fresh threat to strike Iran, raising tensions during high-level talks to find a permanent resolution to the war that’s roiled global markets.

item.title: BOJ Dissent by Takaichi’s Pick Highlights Case for Faster Hikes
item.description: A single dissenting vote against the Bank of Japan’s latest interest rate hike shows the time crunch that hawks are under to get rates to a more normal level before it’s too late.

item.title: Taiwanese Brothers Amass $1 Billion From Boom in Display Chips
item.description: Biing-seng Wu has spent most of his career developing tiny chips that tell pixels on flat-screen displays how and when to light up.

item.title: US Futures Slip, Oil Climbs on Renewed Iran Threat: Markets Wrap
item.description: US stock futures fell and oil climbed as talks between Washington and Tehran were clouded by a renewed threat from President Donald Trump to strike Iran. The pound slipped on speculation around Keir Starmer’s future as UK prime minister.

item.title: Oil Climbs After Fresh Trump Threat as US-Iran Peace Talks Begin
item.description: Oil rose after President Donald Trump threatened strikes on Iran if Hezbollah keeps attacking Israel, raising concerns about progress for peace talks between Washington and Tehran.

item.title: Singapore Dollar Set to Gain Despite Hawkish Fed, Analysts Say
item.description: The Singapore dollar is poised to strengthen against the US dollar in the second half of the year despite a hawkish Federal Reserve boosting sentiment toward the greenback, according to strategists.

item.title: Trump-Backed Lawyer Wins Colombian Election in Preliminary Count
item.description: Conservative lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella narrowly won the preliminary vote count in Colombia’s presidential election, likely sweeping aside Gustavo Petro’s leftist movement and realigning Bogotá with the US.

item.title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
item.description: 米イラン和平協議、ホルムズ原油輸送続く、英首相が近く退任表明か、重要鉱物、日産取締役案に不支持

item.title: Renault Pushback on Nissan Board Picks Targets Creditor Mizuho
item.description: Renault SA’s push against two of Nissan Motor Co.’s appointments to the board effectively pits the Japanese carmaker’s top shareholder against its biggest creditor.

item.title: CRH Near Biggest-Ever Deal For Arcosa, Financial Times Says
item.description: Building materials group CRH Plc. is close to its biggest deal ever to acquire competitor Arcosa Inc., and an agreement could come as soon as next week, the Financial Times reports, citing people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Bond Traders Burned by Fed’s Pivot Look to Prices Gauge, Oil
item.description: Bond traders, recently forced to reposition for the possibility of higher interest rates ahead, are looking to this week’s personal spending data for an early read on whether the market’s newly hawkish stance is warranted.

item.title: Germany, KNDS Family Owners Agree on Stake Sale Ahead of IPO
item.description: Families that own half of tankmaker KNDS NV agreed to sell a 40% stake to the German government, according to people familiar with the matter, paving the way for one of Europe’s key defense companies to go public.
